D2 Vision L2 Understand Atom

The Visual Pathway: Eye to V1

follow signals from each retina through the optic chiasm to the visual cortex

Each visual hemifield maps to the opposite hemisphere. Nasal-retina fibres cross at the chiasm while temporal-retina fibres stay, so the right brain sees the left world.

Place a stimulus in the left or right visual field. Watch which retinal halves respond, which fibres cross at the chiasm, and which hemisphere's V1 lights up.
